summary: sea-bound-siren postinstall exfiltrates AWS Secrets Manager contents to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvest routine targeted at containerized environments. Execution is gated by an anti-analysis check (hostname must match the 12-hex-char Docker container ID shape, cwd must not contain `/tmp/` or `/opt/hscan`, and either `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` must be set or cwd must contain `/app/`). When the gate passes, the script shells out via `child_process` to run `aws sts get-caller-identity`, then iterates `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` and `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value` across `us-east-1`, `eu-west-1`, and `eu-central-1` using the installer's ambient AWS credentials. It also greps `/app` for `HTB{...}` flag strings. The collected JSON is chunked and PUT over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at `http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/<sink>` (with `http://127.0.0.1:30782` as a fallback target). The combination of installer-credential abuse, hardcoded bare-IP HTTP sink, and anti-sandbox/anti-developer gating is a clear supply-chain credential exfiltration attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
